cases.notes: while(true) must trip the interrupt/fuel budget without hanging the host
nested hot loop must be interrupted by CPU budget
regex backtracking must not starve the host
allocation growth should hit memory or CPU limits before host pressure escapes
string doubling should be contained by memory or CPU limits
object graph growth should stay inside realm limits
stack overflow should normalize to RuntimeError rather than panic across FFI
mutual recursion should normalize to RuntimeError rather than panic across FFI

eval is rejected by the static policy scan (layer one); independently, the engine POISONS dynamic evaluation so typeof eval === 'undefined' and calling it is a RuntimeError, with the Function-constructor prototype chain poisoned too so eval cannot be re-reached via `(() => {}).constructor`, never dynamic execution (review 009 P1 / 019 P1, CR-13)

the Function constructor is rejected by the static policy scan (layer one); independently, the engine POISONS dynamic evaluation so typeof Function === 'undefined' AND every function-kind prototype's `constructor` is undefined, so `new Function(...)` and the constructor-chain bypass `(() => {}).constructor('return 1')()` are both a RuntimeError, never dynamic execution (review 009 P1 / 019 P1, CR-13)
dynamic import is outside the strict TypeScript applet profile
globalThis mutation attempts should be caught before execution
Object.prototype writes should be rejected before execution
__proto__ assignment should be rejected before execution
process access is not available to applets and should be policy-scanned
require access is not available to applets and should be policy-scanned
raw fetch is forbidden; network must go through ctx.http/ctx.net later
XMLHttpRequest is forbidden; network must go through the host capability API
tight ctx call loop should trip host-call or CPU budget
